_comment: Clinical decision pathway for Impetigo aligned to NHS Pharmacy First criteria with localized lesion threshold logic.
label: Impetigo

questions.text: How long have you had the skin sores or rash?
Where on your body are the sores? (Select all that apply)
Do the sores look crusty, golden/yellow coloured, or weeping?
questions.clinicalNote: Golden crusting is classic non-bullous impetigo
Blisters suggest bullous impetigo — requires assessment

questions.text: Do you feel systemically unwell or have fever (38°C or above)?
Are lesions rapidly spreading or worsening quickly?
Do you have recurrent impetigo episodes?
Roughly how many lesion clusters do you have?

redFlags.description: Rapidly spreading impetigo with systemic illness
redFlags.message: Spreading skin infection with fever needs same-day urgent medical care.
Fever or systemic illness with skin infection needs urgent same-day assessment.

eligibilityRules.reason: Bullous impetigo — requires GP assessment before treatment
Rapidly spreading lesions require GP or urgent care assessment
Widespread lesion distribution is outside pharmacy pathway
More than 4 lesion clusters requires GP assessment
Systemically unwell patient should not be managed through pharmacy impetigo pathway

eligibilityRules.condition: q3 === true && q4 === false && q7 === false && q6 === false && q5 === false && lesionClusterCount <= 4 && !(Array.isArray(q2) && q2.includes('Widespread across multiple areas'))
eligibilityRules.reason: Localized non-bullous impetigo meets pharmacy eligibility

outcomeRules.reason: Localised non-bullous impetigo — topical treatment appropriate

outcomeRules.condition: q3 === false && q4 === false && q5 === false
outcomeRules.reason: Current symptoms do not clearly match impetigo criteria
Requires GP assessment based on exclusion or uncertain criteria

selfCareAdvice: Keep sores clean. Do not share towels or clothing. Avoid school/work until 48 hours after starting treatment or until sores have crusted over.
pharmacyTreatmentOptions: Fusidic acid 2% cream applied 3 times daily for 5 days (localised)
Flucloxacillin 500mg four times daily for 5 days (widespread)
Clarithromycin 250mg twice daily for 5 days (penicillin allergy)
safetyNetAdvice: Return if: sores spread significantly, fever develops, you feel generally unwell, or there is no improvement after 7 days of treatment.
pathwayPatientDisclaimer: This pathway supports routing for typical impetigo-style skin infection; widespread cellulitis, systemic upset, or non-healing lesions need prompt clinician review and may not match this narrow rule set.
